intro: Aphelandra montana, also known as the Gold Dust Plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Aphelandra montana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
